sit: Your team has the puck in the offensive zone. It's sliding toward the blue line. You're the defenseman. What do you do?
opts: Let the puck slide out of the zone
Skate to the puck and keep it in the zone
Back up toward the blue line to give yourself more room to react
Skate to the front of the net

sit: Your team is forechecking. The opposing D just picked up the puck behind their net. You're the first forward in. What's your goal?
opts: Crash the net hard right away and look for a rebound chance in tight
Put pressure on the D so they can't make an easy breakout pass
Wait at the blue line for your linemates to catch up before forechecking
Skate back to the red line

sit: Your team just lost the puck in the offensive zone. The other team is breaking out. What should every forward on your team do immediately?
opts: Stay high in the offensive zone and wait for a long stretch pass
Turn around and skate back toward your own zone
Stop and call for a pass
Stand still and watch

sit: You're a defender and an attacker is coming at you with the puck. You're backing up. Should you give them a lot of space or a little?
opts: A lot of space — give them room
A steady controlled gap — not too much, not too little
No space — close on them immediately as fast as you can
It doesn't matter

sit: Your defenseman picks up the puck in your own corner. As a winger, where should you immediately skate to?
opts: Into the corner to help battle for the puck
Up the boards — give the D an outlet
Into the slot to screen the goalie
To the far blue line

sit: Your team is on the penalty kill. The power play has set up in your zone. What's your team's main job?
opts: Try to score a shorthanded goal
Keep the puck away from dangerous areas and get it out of your zone
Collapse into a tight box in front of the net and block every shot lane
Pressure the puck carrier hard every single time they touch the puck

sit: The puck is in the offensive zone corner on your left. You're the right winger. You don't have the puck. Where should you be?
opts: Down in the corner helping your teammate fight
On the right side near the net, ready for a cross-ice pass
At the blue line
Heading back to the bench

sit: You receive a pass in the offensive zone with nobody near you. You have lots of time. What should you do before shooting or passing?
opts: Shoot right away before the defenders get close
Look up and read what's happening — find the best play
Skate into the corner and let your winger pick up the puck
Pass backward to your defenseman

sit: You're the puck carrier on a 2-on-1. The lone defender is backing up in the middle, not committing to either side. What do you do?
opts: Pass right away since it's a 2-on-1 — the defender can't block the lane yet
Drive wide to force the defender to fully commit first, then decide
Shoot from where you are
Pull up and regroup at the blue line

sit: You're the defenseman at the offensive blue line. The puck goes to the corner. Your nearest winger is there but about to get pressured. No coverage behind you. Should you pinch?
opts: Yes — go help your winger immediately — crash down fast and support the battle because two players on the puck wins more than one every time
No — hold the blue line. If your winger loses the puck you'll be caught deep.
Go to the net front — crash the crease for a rebound while your winger battles along the boards and let your partner cover the point area
Skate behind the net to support — work down low and be ready to receive a D-to-D pass along the goal line from your winning teammate

sit: You're the right defenseman in the offensive zone. A defender is closing on you hard. Your D partner on the left side is wide open. What do you do?
opts: Hold the puck and try to fight through the pressure yourself
Make a quick D-to-D pass to your partner — they're open and you're not
Fire the puck through the traffic
Chip the puck back down into the corner

sit: You're the defenseman at the point on the power play. The slot is full of traffic. You have a shooting lane to the net. What do you do?
opts: Hold the puck and wait for the traffic in the slot to clear away
Shoot immediately — traffic in front means screens and rebounds
Pass cross-ice to the other point while reading the situation
Skate into the slot with it yourself

sit: Your team is in a 2-1-2 forecheck. The puck is in the corner. As the high forward, where do you position?
opts: Go into the corner and chase the puck hard — win the battle with numbers by joining your teammate so you can pin the puck along the boards
Stay high between the two defensemen — cut off passes and take away the middle
Go straight to the net front — be a screen and look for rebounds while the low forwards battle for the puck along the boards in the corner
Back up to the blue line and wait — let your low forwards work the corner and be ready to support if the puck comes to you up top there

sit: You're forechecking. The D picks up the puck behind the net and looks to the left winger up the wall. What's your angle?
opts: Go straight at the D full speed to force a rushed play
Cut off the pass to the left — force the D to go right instead
Stop and stay in the middle to block a shot back the other way
Drop back to the blue line

sit: Your team is entering the offensive zone. Two defenders are both already back. You're the center. What's the best thing you can do?
opts: Drive hard straight to the net
Peel off and go to the weak side — create a triangle of options
Pull up at the blue line
Follow the puck carrier into the corner and support the battle down low

sit: Your team is in the neutral zone with the puck. The other team has three players sitting back. What's the best way to attack?
opts: Skate straight at them full speed
Delay — use a D-to-D pass or drop pass to find a lane that opens up
Dump the puck in the zone deep
Regroup in your own zone and come back up the exact same side

sit: You have the puck in the corner. A defender has you pinned against the boards. Your winger is at the half-wall. What do you do?
opts: Try to power through the defender on the boards and muscle to the net
Use a quick spin move or chip the puck off the boards to your winger
Pass between the defender's legs
Fall to draw a penalty

sit: You're behind your own net with the puck. A forechecker is coming around the left side. Your left D is covered. Your right D is open. What do you do?
opts: Try to carry the puck out to the left side fast
Spin to the right and find your open defenseman
Stop and wait for the forechecker to commit
Shoot it down the ice

sit: Your team has a power play in a 1-3-1. The puck is at the top. A penalty killer closes on the top player hard. What should the top player do?
opts: Hold the puck and fight straight through the pressure — protect it with your body
Move the puck quickly before the pressure arrives — pass to the half-wall or the other side
Shoot it through the pressure no matter the lane — a shot is a shot
Back the puck up toward center ice so you can regroup and enter again

sit: Your team is on the power play. A shot comes from the point. You're standing in front of the net. What's your job on that shot?
opts: Get out of the goalie's line of sight so they can track the puck
Screen the goalie and be ready to tip or deflect the shot
Skate to the corner to grab the rebound
Drop to the half-wall to be an outlet for a pass out of traffic

sit: You're on the penalty kill. The power play is cycling below the dots but not shooting. When should you challenge?
opts: Pressure every single pass the second it leaves their stick — force mistakes
When they slow down or seem to be setting up a shot — not on every pass
Every time the puck is near the boards
Only when the puck gets to the point

sit: You have the puck in the offensive zone. Two defenders are collapsing on you from both sides. You have no open teammates. What do you do?
opts: Try to deke past both defenders and work your way toward the net alone
Protect the puck along the boards and wait for a teammate to support
Pass into the slot blindly
Shoot from where you are

sit: Your team is cycling in the offensive zone. The defenders are staying tight to the net. What should the cycling players be trying to create?
opts: Holding the puck as long as possible just to keep it away from the other team
A gap or opening — a shot lane, a pass to the slot, or a player breaking to the net
Confusion by passing the puck around as quickly as they can
A penalty call by trying to draw contact

sit: You're low in the corner with the puck. Your winger is at the half-wall. A defender is pressuring you hard from behind. What do you do?
opts: Try to power through and hold the puck against the boards
Bank a quick pass off the boards to your half-wall winger
Spin and skate into the middle ice
Shoot the puck on net from down low in the corner

sit: Your team wins a puck battle in your own corner. The other team's forecheckers are still coming hard. What's the fastest way out?
opts: Hold the puck and wait for them to stop until someone tells you otherwise
Move the puck quickly — first open option up the wall or through
Rim it around the boards right away
Shoot it down the ice for icing

sit: Your D picks up the puck behind your net. One forechecker is coming around the right side. Your right winger is up the wall. Your left winger is covered. What's the breakout pass?
opts: Force a pass across to your left winger even though a defender is on them
Pass to the right winger — they're open and the forechecker has committed to that side
Carry it out yourself
Rim it around the boards

sit: You're the puck carrier on a 2-on-1. The defender commits fully to your teammate. The goalie slides across expecting your pass. What's the right call now?
opts: Pass it across to your teammate as planned
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass back the other way
Pull up and regroup

sit: Your team turns the puck over. The other team has a 2-on-1 and you're the only forward close enough to get back. You can't catch the puck carrier. What do you do?
opts: Skate full speed straight at the puck carrier and try to poke it away from behind
Skate back hard on the weak side — get between the second attacker and the net
Go straight to the net to support your goalie
Peel off to the bench for a quick change

sit: Three attackers enter your zone. They overload the left side — two attackers on the left and one on the right. Both your D are back. How should they adjust?
opts: Both D go to the left to cover the two attackers — when the other team overloads one side, your defensemen should both shift there to match numbers
One D takes the two on the left, the other holds the middle and takes the weak-side attacker — both D communicate
Both D shift to the right weak side — defend the back door because that's where the pass is coming to create a tap-in goal for the third attacker
One D goes behind the net to cover — pre-rotate to cover a pass behind the goal line to take away wraparound plays before they develop at all
